main_event: Approximately 7 military personnel at Brooks AFB, San Antonio, Texas observed bright orange star-sized light traveling from SW to N, climbing out of sight, then returning from NE traveling SW faster and at lower altitude; sighted from 2345 26 February to 0100 27 February 1953.
sighted_object: light larger than a star, orange in color; no sound; initially climbing; later traveling SW at faster speed and lower altitude; one witness described oval flight pattern with a second object; speed estimated at 900 MPH by one observer; varied from dull red to very bright; appeared to circle the moon in a large oval pattern
witness_description: 1st Lt [REDACTED] (author of primary witness statement); S/Sgt [REDACTED] (3 statements); A/1c [REDACTED]; A/2c [REDACTED]; S/Sgt [REDACTED] — all at Brooks AFB; A/1c stated 'about twenty or so others' also observed; reliability unknown
interesting_points: Lt witness initiated own investigation: called Kelly AFB Operations, flight service in Austin, Kelly again, and Randolph AFB — all negative for unusual aircraft activity; Kelly reported a couple conventional a/c in local area but pilot saw nothing unusual
Witness accounts conflict significantly: some saw one object, one saw two circling the moon in oval pattern; A/2c statement noted as 'conflicts with other statements'
One S/Sgt statement describes object shooting left and right and disappearing within one minute then returning; another describes steady oval pattern around moon at 900 MPH
S/Sgt statement (p9) describes seeing object twice: initially traveling east-to-north, then approximately 45 minutes to one hour later saw it again in the west near Kelly AFB
CSV flag B:witnesses_24_not_in_text was haiku hallucination; 7 named witnesses with statements; A/1c mentions 'twenty or so others' but they gave no information
